name: teradata
explanation: The Teradata check connects via the `teradatasql` driver (port 1025 by default) and runs SQL queries (DBC views, ResUsageSpma, AllSpaceV) under a dedicated Datadog database user. While the spec marks only `server` and `database` as required, every supported `auth_mechanism` (TD2, TDNEGO, LDAP, KRB5, JWT) needs at minimum a username plus either a password or a JWT/Kerberos token in `auth_data`. The integration also requires the user to grant SELECT on internal Teradata views before any metric works. None of this can be discovered from a wire probe.
notes: DB driver + user-provisioned credentials (pattern D); also needs `database` as a free-form but required identifier.
